title: Add a tracking side for libraries installing / using sepo
body: @sepo-agent /answer how could that be implemented?  -> for example, is there an easy way to list installed repos based on a github app

nodes.body:
Yes, but with an important distinction: **a GitHub App owner can list installations; an individual installed repo cannot discover all other installed repos.**

Current Sepo has useful hooks for this already:

- Installs copy `.agent/` and `.github/` into each target repo: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md#L11-L18
- Each install carries `.agent/sepo-version.json`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/technical-details/versioning.md#L15-L45
- Auth already supports official hosted app, self-managed GitHub App, PAT, and fallback token: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/setup-guide.md#L10-L27
- The shared auth action currently returns only a repo-scoped resolved token/auth mode: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/actions/resolve-github-auth/action.yml#L25-L31

Implementation approach:

1. Add a maintainer-side “installation registry” job, not a normal per-repo Sepo workflow.
2. Authenticate as the Sepo GitHub App itself using an app JWT.
3. Call GitHub’s `GET /app/installations` endpoint to list app installations. GitHub requires a JWT for this endpoint; installation tokens/PATs do not work for the global app list. Docs: https://docs.github.com/en/rest/apps/apps#list-installations-for-the-authenticated-app
4. For each installation, mint an installation token with `POST /app/installations/{installation_id}/access_tokens`.
5. Use that installation token to call `GET /installation/repositories` and list repos accessible to that installation. Docs: https://docs.github.com/en/rest/apps/installations#list-repositories-accessible-to-the-app-installation
6. For each repo, check whether `.agent/sepo-version.json` exists. That separates “GitHub App installed here” from “Sepo files are actually installed here.”
7. Store a small registry artifact, for example:

```json
{
  "full_name": "owner/repo",
  "installation_id": 123,
  "visibility": "private",
  "repository_selection": "selected",
  "sepo_installed": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "source_ref": "main",
  "source_sha": null,
  "last_seen_at": "2026-05-04T00:00:00Z"
}
```

Where to put it:

- For public/high-level tracking: generate a sanitized `data/sepo-installations.json` or markdown report in this repo.
- For private/internal tracking: keep the full registry in a private backing store, artifact, or private branch because repo names from private installations may be sensitive.
- For a dashboard: render the sanitized registry with GitHub Pages or a simple docs page.

What I would add in this repo:

1. `.agent/src/install-registry.ts` with Octokit helpers:
   - list app installations
   - mint installation tokens
   - list repositories per installation
   - read `.agent/sepo-version.json`
2. `.agent/src/cli/sync-install-registry.ts` for a scheduled/manual command.
3. A workflow like `.github/workflows/agent-install-registry.yml`, probably maintainer-only/scheduled.
4. Docs under `.agent/docs/deployment/` explaining:
   - “App installed” vs “Sepo installed”
   - privacy policy for private repo names
   - required app permissions
   - hosted Sepo app vs bring-your-own-app behavior

For the official hosted Sepo app, this global registry should live on the Sepo side or in this repository’s maintainer automation, because normal customer repos only receive short-lived installation tokens through the OIDC broker. They should not be able to enumerate every other installation.

Main clarification before coding: should the first version expose only aggregate/public-safe stats, or should maintainers have a private full repo-level registry?
